channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: ImpMIA: Leveraging Implicit Bias for Membership Inference Attack
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ImpMIA, a white-box membership inference attack that leverages neural networks' implicit maximum-margin bias to identify training samples without training auxiliary models.
  • Uses KKT optimality conditions and gradient-based reconstruction to find samples whose gradients most strongly reconstruct the trained model parameters.
  • Removes common black-box assumptions (knowledge of training hyperparameters, identical distribution of non-training samples, known training fraction) by requiring only model weights and a superset of candidate data.
  • Demonstrates state-of-the-art performance compared to prior black-box and white-box MIAs in the weight-access setting.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuval Golbari', 'Navve Wasserman', 'Gal Vardi', 'Michal Irani']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'privacy', 'white-box-attack', 'implicit-bias', 'gradient-reconstruction']

item.title: PRISM: Programmatic Reasoning with Image Sequence Manipulation for LVLM Jailbreaking
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces PRISM, an ROP-inspired jailbreak that decomposes a harmful instruction into a sequence of benign visual 'gadgets' plus a coordinating textual prompt so an LVLM composes them into a harmful output.
  • Evaluates extensively on safety benchmarks (SafeBench, MM-SafetyBench) against state-of-the-art LVLMs, reporting very high attack success rates (ASR > 0.90) and up to +0.39 ASR improvement over baselines.
  • Identifies a novel vulnerability in LVLMs' compositional/multi-step reasoning pipeline and argues defenses must secure the entire reasoning process rather than individual inputs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Quanchen Zou', 'Zonghao Ying', 'Moyang Chen', 'Wenzhuo Xu', 'Yisong Xiao', 'Yakai Li', 'Deyue Zhang', 'Dongdong Yang', 'Zhao Liu', 'Xiangzheng Zhang']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial attack', 'multimodal security', 'compositional attacks', 'red teaming']

item.title: When Safety Collides: Resolving Multi-Category Harmful Conflicts in Text-to-Image Diffusion via Adaptive Safety Guidance
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies the issue of 'harmful conflicts' in text-to-image diffusion safety guidance, where averaging safety directions across multiple harm categories can cause mitigation of one harm to amplify others.
  • Proposes Conflict-aware Adaptive Safety Guidance (CASG), a training-free framework with two components: Conflict-aware Category Identification (CaCI) to detect the most aligned harmful category during generation, and Conflict-resolving Guidance Application (CrGA) to apply safety steering only along that category.
  • Works with both latent-space and text-space safety mechanisms and is designed to avoid multi-category interference during generation.
  • Reports state-of-the-art reductions in harmful generation on T2I safety benchmarks, up to a 15.4% decrease in harmful rate compared to existing methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yongli Xiang', 'Ziming Hong', 'Zhaoqing Wang', 'Xiangyu Zhao', 'Bo Han', 'Tongliang Liu']
  • Tags: ['safety-guidance', 'text-to-image', 'diffusion-models', 'harm mitigation', 'adversarial-safety']

item.title: Hallucination Filtering in Radiology Vision-Language Models Using Discrete Semantic Entropy
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes using Discrete Semantic Entropy (DSE) computed from multiple stochastic VLM outputs to detect and filter question-answer pairs likely to hallucinate in radiology VQA.
  • Evaluates DSE on two medical datasets (VQA-Med 2019 and a diagnostic radiology set) with GPT-4o and GPT-4.1; filtering high-DSE items substantially increases reported answer accuracy.
  • Shows DSE thresholding (e.g., DSE > 0.3) can reliably identify semantic inconsistency across repeated outputs and serve as a screening/filtering strategy to reduce clinically harmful hallucinations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Patrick Wienholt', 'Sophie Caselitz', 'Robert Siepmann', 'Philipp Bruners', 'Keno Bressem', 'Christiane Kuhl', 'Jakob Nikolas Kather', 'Sven Nebelung', 'Daniel Truhn']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'model-safety', 'vision-language-models', 'medical-imaging', 'robustness']

item.title: JailBound: Jailbreaking Internal Safety Boundaries of Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces JailBound, a two-stage latent-space jailbreak framework for Vision-Language Models: (1) Safety Boundary Probing to approximate a latent decision boundary in the fusion layer, and (2) Safety Boundary Crossing to jointly optimize adversarial perturbations on image and text inputs to steer internal states toward policy-violating outputs.
  • Highlights limitations of prior jailbreak methods (poor directional guidance, local optima, and decoupled modality attacks) and leverages cross-modal latent representations to improve attack efficacy.
  • Reports strong empirical results across six VLMs: average white-box success 94.32% and black-box 67.28%, outperforming prior state-of-the-art methods.
  • Argues the existence of an implicit safety decision boundary in fusion-layer latent space and exposes a substantive safety vulnerability in current VLMs, calling for improved defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jiaxin Song', 'Yixu Wang', 'Jie Li', 'Rui Yu', 'Yan Teng', 'Xingjun Ma', 'Yingchun Wang']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial_attack', 'vision-language_models', 'latent_space', 'multimodal_security']

item.title: Identifying Memorization of Diffusion Models through $p$-Laplace Analysis: Estimators, Bounds and Applications
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes using p-Laplace operators computed from learned score functions of diffusion models to detect memorized training data (unintended memorization).
  • Introduces a numerical p-Laplace approximation built from estimated scores, with theoretical error bounds and empirical validation.
  • Analyzes structured Gaussian mixture models to derive insights and demonstrates transfer to text-conditioned image generation (text-to-image) in a post-generation regime.
  • Evaluates detection on ~500 memorized prompts (~3000 generated images), showing improved identification of memorization, including cases where conditioning text is unavailable.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jonathan Brokman', 'Itay Gershon', 'Amit Giloni', 'Omer Hofman', 'Roman Vainshtein', 'Hisashi Kojima', 'Guy Gilboa']
  • Tags: ['memorization detection', 'privacy leakage', 'diffusion models', 'score-based models', 'p-Laplace analysis']

item.title: Off-The-Shelf Image-to-Image Models Are All You Need To Defeat Image Protection Schemes
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that off-the-shelf image-to-image generative models (via simple text prompts) can act as generic denoisers to remove imperceptible protective perturbations from images.
  • Evaluates the approach across 8 case studies covering 6 diverse image protection schemes, demonstrating it outperforms specialized attacks while preserving image utility for adversaries.
  • Highlights a widespread vulnerability in current image protection methods and argues future defenses must be benchmarked against attacks using off-the-shelf GenAI models; provides code repository.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xavier Pleimling', 'Sifat Muhammad Abdullah', 'Gunjan Balde', 'Peng Gao', 'Mainack Mondal', 'Murtuza Jadliwala', 'Bimal Viswanath']
  • Tags: ['evasion/denoising attack', 'image protection bypass', 'generative models', 'robustness/defense evaluation', 'adversarial attacks']

item.title: NoLan: Mitigating Object Hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models via Dynamic Suppression of Language Priors
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes whether object hallucinations in large vision-language models arise from the vision encoder or the language decoder, finding they are predominantly caused by strong language priors in the decoder.
  • Proposes NoLan, a training-free decoding framework that dynamically suppresses language priors by comparing output distributions from multimodal versus text-only inputs and adjusting the token distribution accordingly.
  • Demonstrates that NoLan reduces object hallucinations across multiple LVLMs and tasks, with measured improvements on the POPE benchmark (e.g., up to +6.45 for LLaVA-1.5 7B and +7.21 for Qwen-VL 7B).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Lingfeng Ren', 'Weihao Yu', 'Runpeng Yu', 'Xinchao Wang']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'robustness', 'hallucination-mitigation', 'decoding-methods', 'vision-language-models']

item.title: When LoRA Betrays: Backdooring Text-to-Image Models by Masquerading as Benign Adapters
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Masquerade-LoRA (MasqLoRA), a systematic backdoor attack that embeds a hidden cross-modal mapping into text-to-image diffusion models via a standalone LoRA adapter.
  • Attack method: freeze base model, train only low-rank adapter weights on a few "trigger word → target image" pairs so the adapter produces a predefined image when the trigger is present while remaining benign otherwise.
  • Demonstrates high stealth and effectiveness: minimal compute overhead, behaves indistinguishably from benign models absent triggers, and achieves a reported 99.8% attack success rate.
  • Highlights a supply-chain/security threat in the LoRA-sharing ecosystem and the need for dedicated defenses for adapter-based model sharing.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Liangwei Lyu', 'Jiaqi Xu', 'Jianwei Ding', 'Qiyao Deng']
  • Tags: ['backdoor', 'LoRA', 'model backdoor/poisoning', 'supply-chain security', 'text-to-image diffusion']

item.title: Beyond Static Artifacts: A Forensic Benchmark for Video Deepfake Reasoning in Vision Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces FAQ, a large-scale benchmark framing temporal deepfake analysis as a multiple-choice task with three hierarchical levels: Facial Perception, Temporal Deepfake Grounding, and Forensic Reasoning.
  • Evaluates various vision-language models on FAQ and provides FAQ-IT, an instruction-tuning dataset to teach temporal forensic reasoning.
  • Shows that fine-tuning on FAQ-IT improves in-domain and cross-dataset deepfake detection performance; ablations validate key design choices driving temporal reasoning capabilities.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zheyuan Gu', 'Qingsong Zhao', 'Yusong Wang', 'Zhaohong Huang', 'Xinqi Li', 'Cheng Yuan', 'Jiaowei Shao', 'Chi Zhang', 'Xuelong Li']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'video forensics', 'vision-language models', 'benchmark', 'temporal reasoning']

item.title: TranX-Adapter: Bridging Artifacts and Semantics within MLLMs for Robust AI-generated Image Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies attention dilution when fusing artifact (texture-level) and semantic features in MLLMs for AI-generated image (AIGI) detection due to high intra-feature similarity after softmax.
  • Proposes TranX-Adapter, a lightweight fusion module combining Task-aware Optimal-Transport Fusion (uses Jensen-Shannon divergence as cost) to inject artifact info into semantic features, and X-Fusion (cross-attention) to transfer semantic info into artifact features.
  • Demonstrates consistent accuracy improvements (up to +6%) on standard AIGI detection benchmarks across several advanced MLLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wenbin Wang', 'Yuge Huang', 'Jianqing Xu', 'Yue Yu', 'Jiangtao Yan', 'Shouhong Ding', 'Pan Zhou', 'Yong Luo']
  • Tags: ['AI-generated image detection', 'Multimodal LLMs', 'Artifact-semantic fusion', 'Defense/Detection']

item.title: Dynamic Multimodal Activation Steering for Hallucination Mitigation in Large Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes activation patterns in large vision-language models (LVLMs) and finds that truthfulness and visual perception engage different subsets of attention heads, and that truthfulness steering vectors vary by semantic context.
  • Proposes Dynamic Multimodal Activation Steering: a training-free method that builds a semantic-based truthfulness steering vector database and visual perception steering vectors, then dynamically selects and applies context-relevant steering vectors to influential attention heads at inference time.
  • Reports comprehensive experiments showing substantial reductions in hallucinations across multiple models and datasets, outperforming prior state-of-the-art mitigation methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jianghao Yin', 'Qin Chen', 'Kedi Chen', 'Jie Zhou', 'Xingjiao Wu', 'Liang He']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'activation steering', 'model safety', 'vision-language models', 'inference-time defense']

item.title: Adversarial Robustness of Deep Learning-Based Thyroid Nodule Segmentation in Ultrasound
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes and evaluates two black-box adversarial attacks on thyroid nodule segmentation in B-mode ultrasound: Structured Speckle Amplification Attack (SSAA) in spatial domain and Frequency-Domain Ultrasound Attack (FDUA) in Fourier domain.
  • Tests three inference-time defenses—randomized preprocessing with test-time augmentation, deterministic input denoising, and stochastic ensemble inference—and measures impact on U-Net segmentation (DSC, SSIM) using a dataset of 192 nodules.
  • Finds SSAA substantially degrades segmentation (DSC drop ~0.29) but is partially mitigated by preprocessing/denoising; FDUA causes smaller DSC drop (~0.11) but resists all evaluated defenses, highlighting modality-specific robustness challenges.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nicholas Dietrich', 'David McShannon']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-attacks', 'defenses', 'medical-imaging', 'segmentation', 'ultrasound']

item.title: PSF-Med: Measuring and Explaining Paraphrase Sensitivity in Medical Vision Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces PSF-Med, a benchmark of ~19.7k chest X-ray questions with ~92k meaning-preserving paraphrases to measure paraphrase sensitivity in medical VLMs.
  • Finds substantial yes/no answer flip rates (8%–58%) across six models and shows some models remain consistent even without the image, indicating language-prior reliance.
  • Uses sparse autoencoders and causal patching to identify a sparse feature correlated with prompt framing; clamping that feature at inference reduces flip rates by 31% with only a 1.3 percentage-point accuracy cost.
  • Argues that robustness evaluations should assess both paraphrase stability and image grounding, and demonstrates an actionable mitigation that reduces vulnerability to paraphrase-induced flips.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Binesh Sadanandan', 'Vahid Behzadan']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'paraphrase sensitivity', 'benchmark', 'causal patching', 'medical VLMs']

item.title: Spilled Energy in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Reinterprets the final LLM softmax as an Energy-Based Model and decomposes sequence probabilities into interacting EBMs during decoding.
  • Introduces two training-free metrics computed from output logits—spilled energy (cross-step discrepancy) and marginalized energy (single-step measurable)—to localize answer tokens and detect hallucinations.
  • Evaluates these metrics across nine benchmarks and multiple state-of-the-art LLMs (pretrained and instruction-tuned), showing competitive, robust hallucination detection without additional training or probes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Adrian Robert Minut', 'Hazem Dewidar', 'Iacopo Masi']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'energy-based-model', 'model-safety', 'robustness', 'training-free-metrics']

item.title: When Style Breaks Safety: Defending LLMs Against Superficial Style Alignment
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates that superficial style patterns in jailbreak queries (e.g., formatting) inflate attack success rates (ASR) across 36 LLMs and seven benchmarks (ASR inflation).
  • Shows that fine-tuning models on specific styles increases vulnerability to jailbreaks using those styles (superficial style alignment).
  • Proposes SafeStyle, a defense that augments a small amount of safety training data to match fine-tuning style distributions, and empirically outperforms baselines across multiple models, styles, and datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuxin Xiao', 'Sana Tonekaboni', 'Walter Gerych', 'Vinith Suriyakumar', 'Marzyeh Ghassemi']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial prompts', 'defensive training', 'robustness', 'instruction-tuning vulnerability']

item.title: Incentive-Aligned Multi-Source LLM Summaries
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Truthful Text Summarization (TTS), an incentive-aligned pipeline to improve factual robustness of multi-source LLM summaries without ground-truth labels.
  • TTS decomposes drafts into atomic claims, elicits each source's stance per claim, and scores sources with an adapted multi-task peer-prediction mechanism that rewards informative agreement.
  • Unreliable sources are filtered before re-summarization; the paper gives formal guarantees that truthful reporting is utility-maximizing and shows empirical gains in factual accuracy and robustness while preserving fluency.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yanchen Jiang', 'Zhe Feng', 'Aranyak Mehta']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'incentive-alignment', 'adversarial-robustness', 'peer-prediction', 'summarization']

item.title: Probabilistic distances-based hallucination detection in LLMs with RAG
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an unsupervised hallucination detection method for LLMs in retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) by estimating distances between distributions of prompt token embeddings and response token embeddings.
  • Uses geometric structure of token hidden states to extract a factuality signal that scales to long sequences and transfers from NLI to hallucination detection.
  • Reports state-of-the-art or competitive performance across extensive experiments and emphasizes efficiency and transferability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rodion Oblovatny', 'Alexandra Kuleshova', 'Konstantin Polev', 'Alexey Zaytsev']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'retrieval-augmented-generation', 'LLM-safety', 'unsupervised-method']

item.title: Simple Yet Effective: Extracting Private Data Across Clients in Federated Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies cross-client data extraction attacks in federated fine-tuning of large language models, where a semi-honest participant attempts to recover PII from other clients.
  • Proposes three simple attack strategies leveraging contextual prefixes from the attacker's local data (including frequency-based prefix sampling and local fine-tuning to amplify memorization).
  • Constructs a Chinese legal-domain dataset with fine-grained PII annotations and evaluates attacks using coverage and efficiency metrics; code and data released.
  • Finds substantial privacy risk (recovering up to 56.6% of victim-exclusive PII), with names, addresses, and birthdays especially vulnerable.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yingqi Hu', 'Zhuo Zhang', 'Jingyuan Zhang', 'Jinghua Wang', 'Qifan Wang', 'Lizhen Qu', 'Zenglin Xu']
  • Tags: ['data extraction', 'federated learning', 'privacy leakage', 'training data memorization']

item.title: Refusal Direction is Universal Across Safety-Aligned Languages
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a single 'refusal direction' in model activation space that mediates refusal behavior and shows it can be used to bypass refusals (jailbreak) in LLMs.
  • Creates PolyRefuse, a multilingual dataset (14 languages) of malicious and benign prompts, and demonstrates that an English-derived refusal-bypass vector transfers with near-perfect effectiveness to other languages without fine-tuning.
  • Shows refusal directions derived from any safety-aligned language transfer to other languages and attributes this to parallelism of refusal vectors in embedding space, revealing a mechanistic basis for cross-lingual jailbreaks.
  • Discusses implications for designing more robust multilingual safety defenses and provides actionable insights for vulnerability mitigation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xinpeng Wang', 'Mingyang Wang', 'Yihong Liu', 'Hinrich Sch\\"utze', 'Barbara Plank']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'multilingual jailbreaks', 'activation-space intervention', 'safety/defense', 'adversarial vulnerability']

item.title: NoLan: Mitigating Object Hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models via Dynamic Suppression of Language Priors
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes whether object hallucinations in large vision-language models arise from the vision encoder or the language decoder, finding they are predominantly caused by strong language priors in the decoder.
  • Proposes NoLan, a training-free decoding framework that dynamically suppresses language priors by comparing output distributions from multimodal versus text-only inputs and adjusting the token distribution accordingly.
  • Demonstrates that NoLan reduces object hallucinations across multiple LVLMs and tasks, with measured improvements on the POPE benchmark (e.g., up to +6.45 for LLaVA-1.5 7B and +7.21 for Qwen-VL 7B).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Lingfeng Ren', 'Weihao Yu', 'Runpeng Yu', 'Xinchao Wang']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'robustness', 'hallucination-mitigation', 'decoding-methods', 'vision-language-models']

item.title: Alignment-Weighted DPO: A principled reasoning approach to improve safety alignment
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies that LLMs' vulnerability to jailbreaks arises from shallow alignment that rejects harmful prompts without principled reasoning, demonstrated via causal intervention.
  • Creates a Chain-of-Thought (CoT) fine-tuning dataset containing step-by-step rationales for both utility and safety-critical prompts to encourage principled refusals.
  • Proposes Alignment-Weighted DPO, which assigns different preference weights to reasoning and final-answer segments to produce targeted policy updates and improve robustness to jailbreak strategies.
  • Reports extensive experiments showing improved alignment robustness against diverse jailbreaks while preserving overall model utility compared to SFT and vanilla DPO baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mengxuan Hu', 'Vivek V. Datla', 'Anoop Kumar', 'Zihan Guan', 'Sheng Li', 'Alfy Samuel', 'Daben Liu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaks', 'alignment defenses', 'chain-of-thought', 'preference optimization']

item.title: Under the Influence: Quantifying Persuasion and Vigilance in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Empirical study of LLMs as advisors using a multi-turn Sokoban puzzle game to measure persuasion (ability to convince) and vigilance (ability to detect/discard malicious advice).
  • Finds that task performance, persuasive capability, and vigilance are dissociable: good puzzle solvers are not necessarily good at detecting deception.
  • Models change token usage depending on advice benevolence (fewer tokens for benevolent advice, more for malicious), yet can still be persuaded into failure.
  • Argues that monitoring persuasion, vigilance, and task performance independently is important for AI safety and assessing vulnerability to social-engineering-style attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sasha Robinson', 'Kerem Oktar', 'Katherine M. Collins', 'Ilia Sucholutsky', 'Kelsey R. Allen']
  • Tags: ['persuasion', 'social-engineering', 'deception', 'robustness', 'ai-safety']

item.title: Skill-Inject: Measuring Agent Vulnerability to Skill File Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SkillInject, a benchmark of 202 skill-file prompt-injection attacks designed to evaluate LLM agent vulnerability to malicious or hidden instructions in third-party skill files.
  • Measures both security (avoidance of harmful instructions) and utility (compliance with legitimate instructions) across frontier LLM agents, finding up to ~80% attack success including data exfiltration, destructive actions, and ransomware-like behavior.
  • Finds that model scaling and simple input filtering are insufficient defenses and argues for context-aware authorization frameworks and supply-chain-aware agent security.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['David Schmotz', 'Luca Beurer-Kellner', 'Sahar Abdelnabi', 'Maksym Andriushchenko']
  • Tags: ['prompt-injection', 'agent-security', 'benchmark', 'adversarial-attacks', 'supply-chain-security']

item.title: Private Blind Model Averaging - Distributed, Non-interactive, and Convergent
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces and analyzes BlindAvg, a non-interactive (one-shot) distributed differentially private model averaging protocol for convex/smooth ERM, focusing on privacy-utility tradeoffs.
  • Proves BlindAvg converges toward centralized learning under sufficiently strong L2 regularization for non-smooth SVM learners and characterizes utility loss.
  • Proposes SoftmaxReg, a novel differentially private convex smooth ERM learner with improved privacy-utility tradeoff in multi-class settings, and empirically evaluates methods on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and Federated EMNIST (including extreme non-IID ablation).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Moritz Kirschte', 'Sebastian Meiser', 'Saman Ardalan', 'Esfandiar Mohammadi']
  • Tags: ['differential-privacy', 'federated-learning', 'distributed-learning', 'privacy-preserving', 'model-averaging']

item.title: When Fusion Helps and When It Breaks: View-Aligned Robustness in Same-Source Financial Imaging
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies same-source multi-view financial imaging (OHLCV chart and technical-indicator matrix) for next-day direction prediction with an ex-post minimum-movement threshold to reduce label ambiguity.
  • Compares early fusion (channel stacking) versus dual-encoder late fusion with optional cross-branch consistency under leakage-resistant time-block splits.
  • Evaluates pixel-space L-infinity evasion attacks (FGSM/PGD) under view-constrained and joint threat models, finding regime-dependent effects: early fusion can suffer negative transfer in noisy settings, late fusion is more reliable when labels stabilize, and joint perturbations remain challenging.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rui Ma']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'evasion attacks', 'multi-view fusion', 'financial machine learning', 'FGSM/PGD']

item.title: ImpMIA: Leveraging Implicit Bias for Membership Inference Attack
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ImpMIA, a white-box membership inference attack that leverages neural networks' implicit maximum-margin bias to identify training samples without training auxiliary models.
  • Uses KKT optimality conditions and gradient-based reconstruction to find samples whose gradients most strongly reconstruct the trained model parameters.
  • Removes common black-box assumptions (knowledge of training hyperparameters, identical distribution of non-training samples, known training fraction) by requiring only model weights and a superset of candidate data.
  • Demonstrates state-of-the-art performance compared to prior black-box and white-box MIAs in the weight-access setting.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuval Golbari', 'Navve Wasserman', 'Gal Vardi', 'Michal Irani']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'privacy', 'white-box-attack', 'implicit-bias', 'gradient-reconstruction']

item.title: Chasing the Tail: Effective Rubric-based Reward Modeling for Large Language Model Post-Training
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies reward over-optimization (reward hacking) in reinforcement fine-tuning as stemming from reward misspecification at the high-reward tail—difficulty distinguishing Excellent vs. Great responses.
  • Proposes using rubric-based rewards that leverage off-policy exemplars while being robust to their artifacts, enabling better discrimination among high-quality responses.
  • Provides a workflow to elicit rubrics targeted at the high-reward tail and empirically shows rubric-based rewards reduce reward over-optimization and improve LLM post-training.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Junkai Zhang', 'Zihao Wang', 'Lin Gui', 'Swarnashree Mysore Sathyendra', 'Jaehwan Jeong', 'Victor Veitch', 'Wei Wang', 'Yunzhong He', 'Bing Liu', 'Lifeng Jin']
  • Tags: ['reward-overoptimization', 'reward-hacking', 'reinforcement-fine-tuning', 'alignment', 'rubric-based-rewards']

item.title: When Style Breaks Safety: Defending LLMs Against Superficial Style Alignment
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates that superficial style patterns in jailbreak queries (e.g., formatting) inflate attack success rates (ASR) across 36 LLMs and seven benchmarks (ASR inflation).
  • Shows that fine-tuning models on specific styles increases vulnerability to jailbreaks using those styles (superficial style alignment).
  • Proposes SafeStyle, a defense that augments a small amount of safety training data to match fine-tuning style distributions, and empirically outperforms baselines across multiple models, styles, and datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuxin Xiao', 'Sana Tonekaboni', 'Walter Gerych', 'Vinith Suriyakumar', 'Marzyeh Ghassemi']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial prompts', 'defensive training', 'robustness', 'instruction-tuning vulnerability']

item.title: RABot: Reinforcement-Guided Graph Augmentation for Imbalanced and Noisy Social Bot Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes RABot, a graph-augmentation framework for social bot detection that addresses class imbalance and topological noise.
  • Introduces neighborhood-aware oversampling that linearly interpolates minority-class embeddings within local subgraphs to stabilize decision boundaries.
  • Uses a reinforcement-learning-driven edge-filtering module that combines similarity-based edge features with adaptive thresholding to remove spurious interactions.
  • Evaluated across three real-world benchmarks and four GNN backbones, showing consistent improvements and easy integration into existing GNN pipelines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Longlong Zhang', 'Xi Wang', 'Haotong Du', 'Yangyi Xu', 'Zhuo Liu', 'Yang Liu']
  • Tags: ['social-bot-detection', 'graph-neural-networks', 'data-augmentation', 'reinforcement-learning', 'robustness']

item.title: Private and Robust Contribution Evaluation in Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces two secure-aggregation-compatible contribution scores for cross-silo federated learning: Fair-Private (satisfies fairness axioms) and Everybody-Else (removes self-evaluation to resist manipulation).
  • Provides theoretical guarantees for fairness, privacy, robustness, and computational efficiency, addressing both privacy leakage and strategic/malicious client behavior.
  • Empirically evaluates on medical image datasets and CIFAR-10, showing better approximation of Shapley-based rankings, improved downstream model performance, and enhanced misbehavior detection compared to baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Delio Jaramillo Velez', 'Gergely Biczok', 'Alexandre Graell i Amat', 'Johan Ostman', 'Balazs Pejo']
  • Tags: ['federated learning', 'privacy-preserving', 'secure aggregation', 'contribution evaluation', 'robustness / misbehavior detection']

item.title: ConformalHDC: Uncertainty-Aware Hyperdimensional Computing with Application to Neural Decoding
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces ConformalHDC, combining conformal prediction with hyperdimensional computing to provide uncertainty-aware, distribution-free coverage guarantees.
  • Proposes two variants: set-valued conformal sets with finite-sample coverage that create enclosed decision boundaries, and a point-valued variant that uses conformity scores to improve single-label predictions.
  • Claims improved robustness to outliers, non-conforming/OOD inputs and adversarial perturbations via abstention and tighter decision boundaries.
  • Demonstrates the framework on real-world datasets, including neural decoding of hippocampal spike activity, showing accurate decoding plus principled abstention on out-of-distribution behavioral states.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ziyi Liang', 'Hamed Poursiami', 'Zhishun Yang', 'Keiland Cooper', 'Akhilesh Jaiswal', 'Maryam Parsa', 'Norbert Fortin', 'Babak Shahbaba']
  • Tags: ['conformal prediction', 'hyperdimensional computing', 'uncertainty quantification', 'robustness / OOD detection', 'neural decoding']

item.title: PSF-Med: Measuring and Explaining Paraphrase Sensitivity in Medical Vision Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces PSF-Med, a benchmark of ~19.7k chest X-ray questions with ~92k meaning-preserving paraphrases to measure paraphrase sensitivity in medical VLMs.
  • Finds substantial yes/no answer flip rates (8%–58%) across six models and shows some models remain consistent even without the image, indicating language-prior reliance.
  • Uses sparse autoencoders and causal patching to identify a sparse feature correlated with prompt framing; clamping that feature at inference reduces flip rates by 31% with only a 1.3 percentage-point accuracy cost.
  • Argues that robustness evaluations should assess both paraphrase stability and image grounding, and demonstrates an actionable mitigation that reduces vulnerability to paraphrase-induced flips.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Binesh Sadanandan', 'Vahid Behzadan']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'paraphrase sensitivity', 'benchmark', 'causal patching', 'medical VLMs']

item.title: Under the Influence: Quantifying Persuasion and Vigilance in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Empirical study of LLMs as advisors using a multi-turn Sokoban puzzle game to measure persuasion (ability to convince) and vigilance (ability to detect/discard malicious advice).
  • Finds that task performance, persuasive capability, and vigilance are dissociable: good puzzle solvers are not necessarily good at detecting deception.
  • Models change token usage depending on advice benevolence (fewer tokens for benevolent advice, more for malicious), yet can still be persuaded into failure.
  • Argues that monitoring persuasion, vigilance, and task performance independently is important for AI safety and assessing vulnerability to social-engineering-style attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sasha Robinson', 'Kerem Oktar', 'Katherine M. Collins', 'Ilia Sucholutsky', 'Kelsey R. Allen']
  • Tags: ['persuasion', 'social-engineering', 'deception', 'robustness', 'ai-safety']

item.title: INTACT: Intent-Aware Representation Learning for Cryptographic Traffic Violation Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes INTACT, a policy-conditioned (intent-aware) representation learning framework for detecting cryptographic traffic violations by modeling violation probability conditioned on observed behavior and declared security intent.
  • Architecture factorizes into separate behavioral and intent encoders whose fused embeddings yield a violation score, producing a family of policy-parameterized decision boundaries rather than a static anomaly boundary.
  • Evaluated on a real-world network flow dataset and a large synthetic multi-intent cryptographic dataset, showing superior detection (AUROC up to 1.0000 on real data) and improved detection of relational and composite violations versus unsupervised and supervised baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rahul D Ray']
  • Tags: ['intrusion-detection', 'policy-conditioned-detection', 'cryptographic-traffic', 'representation-learning', 'security-monitoring']

item.title: Robustness in sparse artificial neural networks trained with adaptive topology
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies sparse neural networks (three sparse layers at ~99% sparsity + a dense layer) with adaptive topology updated between epochs for image classification (MNIST, FashionMNIST).
  • Evaluates robustness under perturbations including random link removal, adversarial attacks, and link weight shuffling.
  • Finds that adaptive topology maintains competitive accuracy and robustness despite extreme sparsity, suggesting efficiency and resilience benefits.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Bendeg\\'uz Sulyok", 'Gergely Palla', 'Filippo Radicchi', 'Santo Fortunato']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'adversarial attacks', 'sparse neural networks', 'adaptive topology', 'empirical evaluation']

item.title: JSAM: Privacy Straggler-Resilient Joint Client Selection and Incentive Mechanism Design in Differentially Private Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes JSAM, a Bayesian-optimal framework that jointly optimizes client selection probabilities and differential-privacy compensation to maximize FL training effectiveness under a budget constraint.
  • Reduces a high-dimensional optimization into a three-dimensional problem via theoretical characterization; shows servers should favor privacy-tolerant clients and may exclude high-sensitivity 'privacy stragglers'.
  • Evaluates on MNIST and CIFAR-10, reporting up to ~15% test accuracy improvement over unbiased selection mechanisms while maintaining cost efficiency under data heterogeneity.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ruichen Xu', 'Ying-Jun Angela Zhang', 'Jianwei Huang']
  • Tags: ['differential privacy', 'federated learning', 'incentive mechanism', 'client selection', 'privacy-preserving']

item.title: Provably Safe Generative Sampling with Constricting Barrier Functions
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an online safety-filtering framework for pre-trained flow-based generative models that enforces hard constraints during sampling without retraining.
  • Defines a constricting safety tube tightened over the generative process and encodes it with Control Barrier Functions (CBFs), applying a convex QP feedback control at each sampling step.
  • Provides provable guarantees of constraint satisfaction and minimal per-step distributional shift (KL divergence), and demonstrates 100% constraint satisfaction on image generation, trajectory sampling, and robotic manipulation tasks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Darshan Gadginmath', 'Ahmed Allibhoy', 'Fabio Pasqualetti']
  • Tags: ['safety guardrails', 'control barrier functions', 'generative model defense', 'provable guarantees', 'runtime enforcement']

item.title: Bypassing AI Control Protocols via Agent-as-a-Proxy Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces the 'Agent-as-a-Proxy' attack: prompt injections that use an agent to deliver payloads and simultaneously bypass joint CoT-and-tool-use monitoring defenses.
  • Empirically demonstrates that monitoring-based defenses (including large-scale monitors like Qwen2.5-72B) can be bypassed by agents of comparable capability (e.g., GPT-4o mini, Llama-3.1-70B).
  • Evaluates attack success on the AgentDojo benchmark against monitoring systems such as AlignmentCheck and Extract-and-Evaluate, reporting high success rates across diverse monitor LLMs.
  • Concludes that monitoring-based agentic defenses are fundamentally fragile irrespective of monitor scale, raising important security implications for agent oversight.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jafar Isbarov', 'Murat Kantarcioglu']
  • Tags: ['prompt-injection', 'agent-security', 'red-teaming', 'monitoring-bypass', 'adversarial-attacks']

item.title: Incentive-Aligned Multi-Source LLM Summaries
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Truthful Text Summarization (TTS), an incentive-aligned pipeline to improve factual robustness of multi-source LLM summaries without ground-truth labels.
  • TTS decomposes drafts into atomic claims, elicits each source's stance per claim, and scores sources with an adapted multi-task peer-prediction mechanism that rewards informative agreement.
  • Unreliable sources are filtered before re-summarization; the paper gives formal guarantees that truthful reporting is utility-maximizing and shows empirical gains in factual accuracy and robustness while preserving fluency.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yanchen Jiang', 'Zhe Feng', 'Aranyak Mehta']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'incentive-alignment', 'adversarial-robustness', 'peer-prediction', 'summarization']

item.title: Chasing the Tail: Effective Rubric-based Reward Modeling for Large Language Model Post-Training
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies reward over-optimization (reward hacking) in reinforcement fine-tuning as stemming from reward misspecification at the high-reward tail—difficulty distinguishing Excellent vs. Great responses.
  • Proposes using rubric-based rewards that leverage off-policy exemplars while being robust to their artifacts, enabling better discrimination among high-quality responses.
  • Provides a workflow to elicit rubrics targeted at the high-reward tail and empirically shows rubric-based rewards reduce reward over-optimization and improve LLM post-training.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Junkai Zhang', 'Zihao Wang', 'Lin Gui', 'Swarnashree Mysore Sathyendra', 'Jaehwan Jeong', 'Victor Veitch', 'Wei Wang', 'Yunzhong He', 'Bing Liu', 'Lifeng Jin']
  • Tags: ['reward-overoptimization', 'reward-hacking', 'reinforcement-fine-tuning', 'alignment', 'rubric-based-rewards']

item.title: On the Inference (In-)Security of Vertical Federated Learning: Efficient Auditing against Inference Tampering Attack
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes VeFIT, a novel Vertical Federated Inference Tampering attack allowing a malicious data party to covertly manipulate local inference and reduce the task party's accuracy (reported average drop 34.49%).
  • Shows existing defenses fail to detect VeFIT (detection near random), and introduces VeFIA: an auditing framework that uses Trusted Execution Environments (TEE) and a coordinator to validate data-party computation via random sampling without leaking privacy or adding online latency.
  • Provides theoretical and empirical guarantees: if attacked inference proportion >5.4%, VeFIA detects malicious behavior with 99.99% probability; experimental claims of 100% PPV, NPV, and TPR in detecting VeFIT, plus validation on real datasets for privacy and scalability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chung-ju Huang', 'Ziqi Zhang', 'Yinggui Wang', 'Binghui Wang', 'Tao Wei', 'Leye Wang']
  • Tags: ['vertical federated learning', 'inference tampering', 'auditing', 'trusted execution environment', 'attack detection']

item.title: Probabilistic distances-based hallucination detection in LLMs with RAG
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an unsupervised hallucination detection method for LLMs in retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) by estimating distances between distributions of prompt token embeddings and response token embeddings.
  • Uses geometric structure of token hidden states to extract a factuality signal that scales to long sequences and transfers from NLI to hallucination detection.
  • Reports state-of-the-art or competitive performance across extensive experiments and emphasizes efficiency and transferability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rodion Oblovatny', 'Alexandra Kuleshova', 'Konstantin Polev', 'Alexey Zaytsev']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'retrieval-augmented-generation', 'LLM-safety', 'unsupervised-method']

item.title: When Style Breaks Safety: Defending LLMs Against Superficial Style Alignment
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates that superficial style patterns in jailbreak queries (e.g., formatting) inflate attack success rates (ASR) across 36 LLMs and seven benchmarks (ASR inflation).
  • Shows that fine-tuning models on specific styles increases vulnerability to jailbreaks using those styles (superficial style alignment).
  • Proposes SafeStyle, a defense that augments a small amount of safety training data to match fine-tuning style distributions, and empirically outperforms baselines across multiple models, styles, and datasets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuxin Xiao', 'Sana Tonekaboni', 'Walter Gerych', 'Vinith Suriyakumar', 'Marzyeh Ghassemi']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'adversarial prompts', 'defensive training', 'robustness', 'instruction-tuning vulnerability']

item.title: Simple Yet Effective: Extracting Private Data Across Clients in Federated Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies cross-client data extraction attacks in federated fine-tuning of large language models, where a semi-honest participant attempts to recover PII from other clients.
  • Proposes three simple attack strategies leveraging contextual prefixes from the attacker's local data (including frequency-based prefix sampling and local fine-tuning to amplify memorization).
  • Constructs a Chinese legal-domain dataset with fine-grained PII annotations and evaluates attacks using coverage and efficiency metrics; code and data released.
  • Finds substantial privacy risk (recovering up to 56.6% of victim-exclusive PII), with names, addresses, and birthdays especially vulnerable.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yingqi Hu', 'Zhuo Zhang', 'Jingyuan Zhang', 'Jinghua Wang', 'Qifan Wang', 'Lizhen Qu', 'Zenglin Xu']
  • Tags: ['data extraction', 'federated learning', 'privacy leakage', 'training data memorization']

item.title: Spilled Energy in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Reinterprets the final LLM softmax as an Energy-Based Model (EBM) and decomposes sequence-to-sequence probability into interacting EBMs to track "energy spills" during decoding.
  • Proposes two training-free, logits-derived metrics—spilled energy (cross-step discrepancy) and marginalized energy (single-step)—to localize answer tokens and detect hallucinations without probes or retraining.
  • Evaluated on nine benchmarks and multiple state-of-the-art LLMs (LLaMA, Mistral, Gemma, Qwen3), showing competitive hallucination detection and cross-task generalization for pretrained and instruction-tuned models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Adrian Robert Minut', 'Hazem Dewidar', 'Iacopo Masi']
  • Tags: ['hallucination-detection', 'energy-based-models', 'LLM-robustness', 'model-evaluation', 'safety']

item.title: OMNI-LEAK: Orchestrator Multi-Agent Network Induced Data Leakage
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies and red-teams a novel attack (OMNI-LEAK) on orchestrator-style multi-agent LLM systems that causes sensitive data leakage via an indirect prompt injection.
  • Demonstrates the attack against a representative engineered setup with access controls, showing compromise of multiple agents from a single injected prompt.
  • Evaluates susceptibility across frontier models (reasoning and non-reasoning) and shows attacks succeed even without insider knowledge of implementation details.
  • Argues that safety research must extend from single-agent to multi-agent settings to mitigate privacy breaches, financial risk, and erosion of trust.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Akshat Naik', 'Jay Culligan', 'Yarin Gal', 'Philip Torr', 'Rahaf Aljundi', 'Alasdair Paren', 'Adel Bibi']
  • Tags: ['multi-agent', 'prompt injection', 'data leakage', 'red teaming', 'LLM security']

item.title: 1-2-3 Check: Enhancing Contextual Privacy in LLM via Multi-Agent Reasoning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a multi-agent framework that splits privacy reasoning into specialized subtasks (e.g., extraction, classification) to limit information exposure to any single agent and enable iterative validation.
  • Performs systematic ablations over information-flow topologies to analyze how upstream detection errors propagate and cause downstream private-information leakage.
  • Evaluates on ConfAIde and PrivacyLens with multiple LLMs (including GPT-4o), reporting substantial reductions in private information leakage (~18% on ConfAIde and ~19% on PrivacyLens) while maintaining public-content fidelity versus single-agent baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wenkai Li', 'Liwen Sun', 'Zhenxiang Guan', 'Xuhui Zhou', 'Maarten Sap']
  • Tags: ['privacy-defense', 'multi-agent-systems', 'information-flow', 'leakage-mitigation']

item.title: Off-The-Shelf Image-to-Image Models Are All You Need To Defeat Image Protection Schemes
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that off-the-shelf image-to-image generative models (via simple text prompts) can act as generic denoisers to remove imperceptible protective perturbations from images.
  • Evaluates the approach across 8 case studies covering 6 diverse image protection schemes, demonstrating it outperforms specialized attacks while preserving image utility for adversaries.
  • Highlights a widespread vulnerability in current image protection methods and argues future defenses must be benchmarked against attacks using off-the-shelf GenAI models; provides code repository.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xavier Pleimling', 'Sifat Muhammad Abdullah', 'Gunjan Balde', 'Peng Gao', 'Mainack Mondal', 'Murtuza Jadliwala', 'Bimal Viswanath']
  • Tags: ['evasion/denoising attack', 'image protection bypass', 'generative models', 'robustness/defense evaluation', 'adversarial attacks']

item.title: NoLan: Mitigating Object Hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models via Dynamic Suppression of Language Priors
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes whether object hallucinations in large vision-language models arise from the vision encoder or the language decoder, finding they are predominantly caused by strong language priors in the decoder.
  • Proposes NoLan, a training-free decoding framework that dynamically suppresses language priors by comparing output distributions from multimodal versus text-only inputs and adjusting the token distribution accordingly.
  • Demonstrates that NoLan reduces object hallucinations across multiple LVLMs and tasks, with measured improvements on the POPE benchmark (e.g., up to +6.45 for LLaVA-1.5 7B and +7.21 for Qwen-VL 7B).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Lingfeng Ren', 'Weihao Yu', 'Runpeng Yu', 'Xinchao Wang']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'robustness', 'hallucination-mitigation', 'decoding-methods', 'vision-language-models']

item.title: Resilient Federated Chain: Transforming Blockchain Consensus into an Active Defense Layer for Federated Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Resilient Federated Chain (RFC), a blockchain-enabled federated learning framework that repurposes pooled mining redundancy as an active defense layer.
  • Introduces a flexible consensus evaluation function and integrates robust aggregation rules to adaptively mitigate various adversarial attacks in FL.
  • Evaluates RFC on image classification tasks under multiple adversarial scenarios and reports improved robustness compared to baseline methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ["Mario Garc\\'ia-M\\'arquez", "Nuria Rodr\\'iguez-Barroso", "M. Victoria Luz\\'on", 'Francisco Herrera']
  • Tags: ['Federated Learning', 'Blockchain-enabled Defense', 'Adversarial Robustness', 'Robust Aggregation', 'Consensus Mechanisms']

item.title: Beyond Static Artifacts: A Forensic Benchmark for Video Deepfake Reasoning in Vision Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces FAQ, a large-scale benchmark framing temporal deepfake analysis as a multiple-choice task with three hierarchical levels: Facial Perception, Temporal Deepfake Grounding, and Forensic Reasoning.
  • Evaluates various vision-language models on FAQ and provides FAQ-IT, an instruction-tuning dataset to teach temporal forensic reasoning.
  • Shows that fine-tuning on FAQ-IT improves in-domain and cross-dataset deepfake detection performance; ablations validate key design choices driving temporal reasoning capabilities.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zheyuan Gu', 'Qingsong Zhao', 'Yusong Wang', 'Zhaohong Huang', 'Xinqi Li', 'Cheng Yuan', 'Jiaowei Shao', 'Chi Zhang', 'Xuelong Li']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'video forensics', 'vision-language models', 'benchmark', 'temporal reasoning']

item.title: Dynamic Multimodal Activation Steering for Hallucination Mitigation in Large Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes activation patterns in large vision-language models (LVLMs) and finds that truthfulness and visual perception engage different subsets of attention heads, and that truthfulness steering vectors vary by semantic context.
  • Proposes Dynamic Multimodal Activation Steering: a training-free method that builds a semantic-based truthfulness steering vector database and visual perception steering vectors, then dynamically selects and applies context-relevant steering vectors to influential attention heads at inference time.
  • Reports comprehensive experiments showing substantial reductions in hallucinations across multiple models and datasets, outperforming prior state-of-the-art mitigation methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jianghao Yin', 'Qin Chen', 'Kedi Chen', 'Jie Zhou', 'Xingjiao Wu', 'Liang He']
  • Tags: ['hallucination mitigation', 'activation steering', 'model safety', 'vision-language models', 'inference-time defense']

item.title: Adversarial Robustness of Deep Learning-Based Thyroid Nodule Segmentation in Ultrasound
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes and evaluates two black-box adversarial attacks on thyroid nodule segmentation in B-mode ultrasound: Structured Speckle Amplification Attack (SSAA) in spatial domain and Frequency-Domain Ultrasound Attack (FDUA) in Fourier domain.
  • Tests three inference-time defenses—randomized preprocessing with test-time augmentation, deterministic input denoising, and stochastic ensemble inference—and measures impact on U-Net segmentation (DSC, SSIM) using a dataset of 192 nodules.
  • Finds SSAA substantially degrades segmentation (DSC drop ~0.29) but is partially mitigated by preprocessing/denoising; FDUA causes smaller DSC drop (~0.11) but resists all evaluated defenses, highlighting modality-specific robustness challenges.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nicholas Dietrich', 'David McShannon']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-attacks', 'defenses', 'medical-imaging', 'segmentation', 'ultrasound']

item.title: Provably Safe Generative Sampling with Constricting Barrier Functions
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an online safety-filtering framework for pre-trained flow-based generative models that enforces hard constraints during sampling without retraining.
  • Defines a constricting safety tube tightened over the generative process and encodes it with Control Barrier Functions (CBFs), applying a convex QP feedback control at each sampling step.
  • Provides provable guarantees of constraint satisfaction and minimal per-step distributional shift (KL divergence), and demonstrates 100% constraint satisfaction on image generation, trajectory sampling, and robotic manipulation tasks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Darshan Gadginmath', 'Ahmed Allibhoy', 'Fabio Pasqualetti']
  • Tags: ['safety guardrails', 'control barrier functions', 'generative model defense', 'provable guarantees', 'runtime enforcement']

item.title: Alignment-Weighted DPO: A principled reasoning approach to improve safety alignment
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies that LLMs' vulnerability to jailbreaks arises from shallow alignment that rejects harmful prompts without principled reasoning, demonstrated via causal intervention.
  • Creates a Chain-of-Thought (CoT) fine-tuning dataset containing step-by-step rationales for both utility and safety-critical prompts to encourage principled refusals.
  • Proposes Alignment-Weighted DPO, which assigns different preference weights to reasoning and final-answer segments to produce targeted policy updates and improve robustness to jailbreak strategies.
  • Reports extensive experiments showing improved alignment robustness against diverse jailbreaks while preserving overall model utility compared to SFT and vanilla DPO baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mengxuan Hu', 'Vivek V. Datla', 'Anoop Kumar', 'Zihan Guan', 'Sheng Li', 'Alfy Samuel', 'Daben Liu']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaks', 'alignment defenses', 'chain-of-thought', 'preference optimization']

item.title: A Systematic Review of Algorithmic Red Teaming Methodologies for Assurance and Security of AI Applications
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematic review of automated/algorithmic red teaming approaches that use AI and automation to simulate attacks and evaluate security of systems.
  • Synthesizes methodologies, tools, benefits, limitations, and trends in automated red teaming for proactive vulnerability discovery and assurance.
  • Identifies challenges and research gaps and outlines future directions for improving automated red teaming as part of cybersecurity strategies.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shruti Srivastava', 'Kiranmayee Janardhan', 'Shaurya Jauhari']
  • Tags: ['automated-red-teaming', 'red-teaming', 'vulnerability-assessment', 'adversarial-testing', 'security-evaluation']
